title: identity: secret_bytes() doc says 'never log' but no compiler enforcement; pattern propagates to callers
description: Identity::secret_bytes() and hpke_secret_bytes() are documented with SECURITY comments but return plain [u8;32]. Any future caller can accidentally log, serialize, or display these values. The zeroize issue (nie-kef6.103) covers the zeroize aspect; this separate issue covers the propagation risk: callers of these functions return their own plain arrays, and new contributors can copy the pattern without noticing the SECURITY annotation. Consider wrapping the return type in a newtype that implements neither Debug nor Display, making accidental logging a compile error.
